launch_cutline.description: A lane may publish as Stable (or LTS) only when its qualification state holds the claim with current, owner-signed proof. Any lane that is not qualified, has stale evidence, relied on an expired waiver, or — for a cross-binary boundary — cannot publish complete mixed-version negotiation data drops below this cutline and never inherits an adjacent green row.

downgrade_rules.title: Lane lacks qualification proof
downgrade_rules.rationale: A lane put forward as Stable with no qualification proof is narrowed below the cutline and holds the stable train until proof lands.

downgrade_rules.title: Lane evidence no longer current
downgrade_rules.rationale: Stable wording cannot ride evidence that has aged out of its freshness window; the packet must be refreshed before promotion proceeds.

downgrade_rules.title: Evidence freshness window exceeded
downgrade_rules.rationale: When the declared freshness window has elapsed the lane stops being claim-bearing and promotion holds until a current packet is recorded.

downgrade_rules.title: Provisional waiver expired
downgrade_rules.rationale: A claim that only held because of a waiver must narrow the moment the waiver expires; the stable train holds rather than silently widening.

downgrade_rules.title: Lane lacks owner sign-off
downgrade_rules.rationale: No stable claim widens without a named owner sign-off; an unsigned lane holds promotion until the owner signs.

downgrade_rules.title: Backing stable claim narrowed below cutline
downgrade_rules.rationale: A qualification lane that grounds itself on a stable claim narrows when that claim drops below the stable cutline, so this matrix never outruns the claim matrix it ingests.

downgrade_rules.title: Mixed-version negotiation data incomplete
downgrade_rules.rationale: A cross-binary boundary that does not publish negotiated fields, a supported skew window, an upgrade order, a rollback order, and the unsupported-state behavior is coordinated-upgrade-only and may not inherit a Stable mixed-version claim; promotion holds until the data lands or the claim is narrowed.

downgrade_rules.title: Operated outside the supported skew window
downgrade_rules.rationale: A boundary observed running outside its declared skew window narrows its mixed-version claim and routes to a coordinated upgrade rather than leaving an unsupported mix presented as Stable.

rows.title: Desktop launcher and local sidecars

supported_skew_window.summary: The launcher and local helper binaries come from one coordinated artifact set with shared build identity, contract version, and schema epoch; rolling skew between them is not a claim on this boundary.

upgrade_order.notes: The launcher and helpers promote together as one coordinated artifact set; there is no partial-upgrade step.

rollback_order.notes: Rollback restores the prior coordinated set, never a partial mix of launcher and helper builds.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: A launcher started against a helper from a different artifact set is refused, not warned; the user is routed to repair the coordinated set.

mixed_version.rationale: The launcher/local-sidecar boundary is honestly coordinated-upgrade-only: the binaries ship and roll back as one set, and that posture is fully declared, so the desktop lane holds Stable with complete mixed-version data.

rows.rationale: The desktop launcher/local-sidecar lane carries current, owner-signed proof and a complete coordinated-upgrade-only mixed-version section, so it qualifies as Stable.

rows.title: Desktop/CLI and the remote agent/helper

supported_skew_window.summary: The desktop/CLI client and the remote agent interoperate within one minor version; the newer side negotiates down to the older peer's contract epoch and capability set.

upgrade_order.notes: Upgrade the remote agent first; the older client negotiates down to the new agent within the one-minor window.

rollback_order.notes: Roll the client back first, then the agent, so the negotiated contract epoch is never left ahead of the surviving peer.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: Outside the one-minor window the session is read-only and prompts a coordinated upgrade rather than running an unsupported mix.

mixed_version.rationale: The desktop/CLI ↔ remote-agent boundary publishes a bounded one-minor skew window with full negotiation, upgrade, rollback, and out-of-window data, so it holds a Stable mixed-version claim.

rows.rationale: The remote agent/helper lane carries current, owner-signed proof and a complete bounded-skew mixed-version section, so it qualifies as Stable with a genuine mixed-version claim.

rows.title: Desktop/CLI/browser and the managed control plane

supported_skew_window.summary: Intended rolling window where managed clients trail the control plane across minor versions; the window is not yet pinned in the version-skew register.

upgrade_order.notes: The control-plane-first upgrade order has not been declared because the managed rollout contract is still pending.

rollback_order.notes: The control-plane rollback order has not been declared; managed-tenant rollback semantics are still under design.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: Provisional: a client ahead of the control plane is expected to degrade, but the contract is not yet ratified.

mixed_version.rationale: The managed-control-plane boundary is put forward as rolling-skew but has not declared its upgrade order or rollback order, so its negotiation data is incomplete; it narrows to coordinated-upgrade-only and may not inherit a Stable mixed-version claim.

rows.rationale: The managed-control-plane lane claimed a rolling mixed-version posture it cannot back: the upgrade and rollback order are undeclared, so the boundary is coordinated-upgrade-only and the lane is narrowed to beta with the mixed-version-incomplete reason holding promotion.

rows.title: Extension host and ABI

supported_skew_window.summary: Extensions built against the current or previous ABI epoch load within the host's declared contract set; unknown required contracts are refused.

upgrade_order.notes: Upgrade the host first; extensions on the current or previous ABI epoch keep loading within the window.

rollback_order.notes: Roll extensions back to a host-compatible ABI epoch before rolling the host back.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: An extension declaring a required contract the host does not provide is refused load rather than partially activated.

mixed_version.rationale: The extension host/ABI boundary publishes complete negotiation data, but the lane's qualification evidence aged out of its freshness window, so the surface narrows to preview and its mixed-version claim drops to coordinated-upgrade-only until the evidence is rerun.

rows.rationale: The extension host/ABI lane's qualification packet aged out of its freshness window, so the lane narrows to preview; the mixed-version section is complete but cannot carry a Stable mixed-version claim while the surface is below the cutline.

rows.title: Saved-artifact and schema readers and writers

upgrade_order.notes: Intended reader-before-writer order so an older reader is never asked to read a newer artifact, pending the forward/backward read drill.

rollback_order.notes: Intended writer-before-reader rollback order, pending the forward/backward read drill.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: A reader encountering a newer schema epoch opens read-only, but the supported skew window backing this rule is not yet declared.

mixed_version.rationale: The saved-artifact/schema reader-writer boundary has not declared a supported skew window in the version-skew register, so its negotiation data is incomplete; it is coordinated-upgrade-only and may not inherit a Stable mixed-version claim.

rows.rationale: No forward/backward reader-writer skew drill has been captured and the boundary's supported skew window is undeclared, so the state/schema lane is narrowed to beta with both the qualification-gap and mixed-version-incomplete reasons holding promotion.

rows.title: Provider adapters

waiver.reason: The provider-adapter negotiation contract and skew window are current; an active waiver covers the still-scheduled cross-provider conformance rerun while it is staffed.

supported_skew_window.summary: Each provider adapter supports the current and previous published provider API minor; outside that the adapter degrades to a read-only capability probe.

upgrade_order.notes: Upgrade the adapter to the new provider API minor before features depend on new capabilities.

rollback_order.notes: Roll dependent features back before the adapter so no feature requests a capability the rolled-back adapter no longer negotiates.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: A provider API outside the supported two-minor window degrades the adapter to a capability probe and surfaces a coordinated-upgrade prompt.

mixed_version.rationale: The provider-adapter boundary publishes a complete bounded-skew window with full negotiation, upgrade, rollback, and out-of-window data, so it carries a Stable mixed-version claim held under the active conformance-rerun waiver.

rows.rationale: The provider-adapter lane holds a Stable claim under an active, unexpired waiver covering the scheduled conformance rerun; its mixed-version section is complete, so the waiver and its expiry are recorded and the claim narrows automatically if it lapses.

rows.title: Accessibility across touched surfaces
rows.rationale: The accessibility lane is a cross-cutting quality row, not a cross-binary boundary, so it carries no mixed-version section; it holds Stable on current keyboard, screen-reader, IME/grapheme/bidi, zoom, high-contrast, and reduced-motion fixtures with an owner sign-off.

promotion.rationale: Three lanes put forward as Stable are narrowed below the cutline: the managed control plane and the state/schema reader-writer boundary cannot publish complete mixed-version negotiation data, and the extension host/ABI lane's evidence aged out. Their reasons fire four blocking downgrade rules, so the stable train is held until they qualify or their claims are formally narrowed.
